title: galatella asperella
intro: Galatella asperella, also known as rough cudweed or rough galatella, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) with a sprawling habit and small, yellow or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, relieving respiratory problems, and reducing inflammation. Herbal preparations of Galatella asperella include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to support overall well-being.
